data.title: US Stocks Brief: S&P Futures Down Near 1% and Nasdaq As President Trump Says Economy Faces "Transition" and Treasury Sec. Scott Bessent Says "There's Going To Be a Detox Period"
Gold steady as firm dollar offsets safe-haven demand
data.description:
Key points:
  • Gold could correct further, analyst says
  • US CPI data due on Wednesday, PPI data on Thursday
  • China's deflationary pressures deepen in February

Gold steadied on Monday as a stronger U.S. dollar countered safe-haven demand amid trade war concerns, while investors looked to inflation data this week for clues on the Federal Reserve's next interest rate decision.

Spot gold was at $2,913.09 an ounce at 0946 GMT, while U.S. gold futures firmed 0.2% to $2,920.10.

The dollar index held above last week's four-month low, making gold more expensive for holders of other currencies.

Quantitative Commodity Research analyst Peter Fertig said a rise in the dollar is weighing on bullion and he expects a further correction to below $2,900.

Meanwhile, market focus remains on trade tensions. In his latest warning to Canada, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Friday that reciprocal tariffs on dairy and lumber could be imminent.

Gold's success in holding above $2,900 reflects concerns about the wider economic picture and an ongoing heightened geopolitical risk environment, Frank Watson, market analyst at Kinesis Money, said in a note.

Traders are looking to U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) data on Wednesday and the Producer Price Index (PPI) on Thursday for U.S. interest rate cues.

The Fed has held interest rates so far this year after cutting three times in 2024. Market pricing reflects expectations of a further cut in June. (FEDWATCH)

Bullion is seen as a hedge against inflation and geopolitical uncertainty but higher rates can dent the non-yielding asset's appeal.

Data showed top metals consumer China's consumer price index missed expectations in February and fell at the sharpest pace in 13 months, while producer price deflation persisted.

Spot silver edged up 0.2% to $32.59 an ounce, platinum rose 1% to $973 and palladium was up 0.5% at $952.68.

About TBC Bank Group PLC ("TBC PLC")

TBC Bank Group's mission is to make people's lives easier by providing digitally-led financial services to retail and corporate customers. TBC Bank Group PLC ("TBC PLC") is a public limited company registered in England and Wales and is the parent company of TBC Bank Georgia and TBC Uzbekistan.

TBC Bank Georgia, together with its subsidiaries, is the leading financial services group in Georgia, with a total market share of 38.5% of customer loans and 38.1% of customer deposits as of 31 December 2024, according to data published by the National Bank of Georgia.

TBC Uzbekistan is the leading digital banking ecosystem in Central Asia with 18 mln unique registered users that includes TBC Bank Uzbekistan, the country's largest mobile-only bank, Payme, a leading digital payments app for individuals and small businesses, and Payme Nasiya, an instalment credit business.

TBC PLC is listed on the London Stock Exchange under the symbol TBCG and is a constituent of the FTSE 250 Index. It is also a member of the FTSE4Good Index Series and the MSCI United Kingdom Small Cap Index.

data.title: DoorDash, TKO, Williams-Sonoma Set to Join S&P 500
data.description:
Palantir Technologies , Intuitive Surgical , and ServiceNow will replace Dow , Kraft Heinz and Ford Motor in the S&P 100, S&P Global's Dow Jones Indices said late Friday.

DoorDash , S&P MidCap 400 constituents TKO , Williams-Sonoma , and Expand Energy , will replace Borgwarner , Teleflex , Celanese , and FMC in the S&P 500. S&P SmallCap 600 constituents VF , Alaska Air , and Hims & Hers Health will replace TKO, Williams-Sonoma and Expand Energy in the S&P MidCap 400.

Teleflex, Celanese, FMC and Borgwarner will replace VF, Alaska Air, Hims & Hers Health, and Ambac Financial in the S&P SmallCap 600.

SmallCap 600 firms Bath & Body Works , ATI , and EchoStar will replace Chemours , Teradata , and Neogen in the S&P MidCap 400. Chemours, Teradata and Neogen will replace Bath & Body Works, ATI, and EchoStar in the S&P SmallCap 600.

Ryman Hospitality Properties , Element Solutions , Freshpet , WillScot, , Mueller Water Products , Kratos Defense & Security Solutions , and CleanSpark will replace Wabash National , Green Dot , Fulgent Genetics , Nabors Industries , Green Plains , Mativ Holdings , and Hain Celestial in the S&P SmallCap 600.

Dow Jones Indices said the changes are effective prior to the open of trading March 24.

data.title: Checkpoint Therapeutics rises on $355 million Sun Pharma buyout deal
data.description:
** Drug developer Checkpoint Therapeutics' shares rise 66% to $4.10 premarket

** India's Sun Pharmaceutical Industries will buy Checkpoint Therapeutics for $355 million, the companies said on Sunday

** Upfront cash payment of $4.10 per share, at a 66% premium to stock's last close

** CKPT's controlling arm Fortress Biotech would receive royalty payments based on future sales of co's skin cancer drug Unloxcyt during a specified term

** FBIO shares rise 12.3% to $1.73 before the bell

** The deal expected to be completed in Q2 2025

** CKPT has risen 13.8% in the last 12 months

data.title: US Stocks Brief: Follows Biggest Losing Week of Year To Date
Slovenia Industrial Output Rises for 2nd Month
data.description:
Industrial production in Slovenia rose by 3.4% year-on-year in January 2025 from a downwardly revised 0.9% increase in the previous month.

This marked the second consecutive month of growth in industrial activity, as output rebounded sharply for electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply (11.4% vs -21.6% in December).

At the same time, production increased further for manufacturing (2.1% vs 1.5%).

Meanwhile, output growth slowed for mining and quarrying (26.7% vs 30.6%).

On a monthly basis, industrial activity advanced by 1.6% in January, following a 0.6% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: European Natural Gas Prices Ease as EU Postpones Plans to Reduce Reliance on Russian Energy Imports, Commerzbank Says
data.description:
European natural gas prices last week fell below 40 euros/megawatt-hour for the first time since the end of September amid milder temperatures and the postponement of the European Union's plans to cut further the bloc's reliance on Russian energy imports, Commerzbank said in a Friday note.

This comes as a relief as dependence from Russia on the gas market remains high despite a major reduction in pipeline supplies, the bank noted. Liquefied natural gas imports from Russia even rose slightly last year.

Meanwhile, EU Commissioner Dan Jorgensen has formulated a recommendation on storage level requirements amid high demand for replenishment, proposing that EU member states should hit a level of 90% by Nov. 1.

However, there is scope to postpone that deadline to Dec. 1 if sufficient justification is provided. Large LNG storage facilities can also be added to the filling levels.

EU gas storage facilities are currently at 37% of capacity. The need to replenish stocks for the coming winter is likely to be around 30 billion cubic feet higher than last year, Commerzbank said.

As a result, the bank believes it is likely that the situation on the European gas market will become tighter again in the course of the year.

Commerzbank maintained its price forecast of 50 euros/MWh at the end of the second quarter and in the second half of the year.

data.title: Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on American Electric Power to $106 From $93, Maintains Neutral Rating
data.description: American Electric Power has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $103.93, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Franco-Nevada's Q4 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Increase
data.description:
Franco-Nevada reported Q4 adjusted earnings Monday of $0.95 per share, up from $0.90 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.90.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $321 million, compared with $303.3 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $304.4 million.

The company's shares rose more than 1% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: DoorDash, TKO Group Holdings, Williams-Sonoma rise on addition to S&P 500
data.description:
** DoorDash's shares rise 5% to $186.93 premarket after announcement co set to join S&P 500

** Additions to occur prior to open of trading on March 24 as part of quarterly rebalance, S&P Dow Jones Indices said on Friday

** TKO Group Holdings , Williams-Sonoma and Expand Energy to also join S&P 500; shares up between 1.6% and 2.9% in low trading volumes

** Cos replace Borgwarner , Teleflex , Celanese Corp and FMC Corp in the benchmark index

** Shares of Palantir Technologies , Intuitive Surgical , ServiceNow , which are also set to join S&P 100, slip amid broader market selloff

** WillScot Holdings up 4.9%, Ryman Hospitality Properties up 4.5% as cos to join S&P SmallCap 600 (.SPCY)

** Bath & Body Works dips 2.2% as co to join S&P 500 MidCap 400

** DASH up 6.1% YTD through Friday close
